meta.description: WebLens RAG benchmark — 30 single-turn questions across diverse domains, stress-testing routing, decomposition, retrieval, grounding, faithfulness, temporal awareness, ambiguity, contradiction, refusal.

questions.question: What is 12 squared?
questions.expected_behavior: Answer 144 directly without web search.
questions.ground_truth: 12 squared is 144 (12 × 12).

questions.question: What is the capital of Brazil?
questions.expected_behavior: Answer Brasília directly. Stable since 1960.
questions.ground_truth: Brasília has been the capital of Brazil since 1960.

questions.question: Who wrote One Hundred Years of Solitude?
questions.expected_behavior: Answer Gabriel García Márquez directly.
questions.ground_truth: Gabriel García Márquez wrote One Hundred Years of Solitude, published in 1967.

questions.question: What is a hash table and what is its average-case lookup complexity?
questions.expected_behavior: Textbook CS — define hash table + O(1) avg lookup, O(n) worst case.
questions.ground_truth: A hash table is a key-value data structure that uses a hash function to compute an index into an array of buckets. Average-case lookup, insert, and delete are O(1); worst case is O(n) when collisions degrade performance.

questions.question: What is the current population of Brazil?
questions.expected_behavior: Numbers drift — must route to search, not parametric.
questions.ground_truth: Population estimates for Brazil in 2025-2026 are around 217 million (varies by source).

questions.question: Who won the FIFA World Cup in 2022?
questions.expected_behavior: Specific recent result, must cite source even if LLM knows.
questions.ground_truth: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup in Qatar, beating France on penalties. Messi captained the team.

questions.question: What's the latest Anthropic Claude model as of mid-2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Recent product release — must search; LLM may not know cutoff.
questions.ground_truth: As of May 2026, Anthropic's frontier Claude family includes Opus 4.7 and Sonnet 4.6, with Haiku 4.5 as the fast tier.

questions.question: Compare GPT-4o, Claude Opus 4.7, and Gemini 2.5 Pro on long-context handling and agentic tool use.
questions.expected_behavior: Should decompose into per-model or per-dimension sub-queries.
questions.ground_truth: All three offer large context windows (≥128k tokens). Claude Opus 4.7 emphasizes extended tool-use and agentic workflows; GPT-4o has multimodal+tool capabilities; Gemini 2.5 Pro has very large context and code/agent reasoning.

questions.question: Compare Real Madrid and Manchester City Champions League performance over the last 3 seasons.
questions.expected_behavior: Per-club, per-season decomposition.
questions.ground_truth: Real Madrid won the 2023-24 Champions League. Manchester City won 2022-23. Both have been semifinalists or better in most recent seasons.

questions.question: How do NVIDIA, AMD, and Intel data-center segments compare in FY2024 revenue and growth?
questions.expected_behavior: Per-company sub-queries.
questions.ground_truth: NVIDIA Data Center FY2024 revenue was ~$47.5B (massive growth). AMD Data Center segment ~$6.5B (strong growth). Intel Data Center & AI ~$15.5B (slight decline).

questions.question: Compare CRISPR-Cas9 and base editing in precision, off-target rates, and therapeutic applications.
questions.expected_behavior: Decompose by dimension (precision / off-target / therapeutic).
questions.ground_truth: CRISPR-Cas9 makes double-strand breaks; base editors (cytosine/adenine) modify single bases without DSBs, generally lower off-target effects. Both have therapeutic applications in sickle-cell, beta-thalassemia, etc.

questions.question: How do the US, EU, and China approach AI regulation in 2025-2026, and where do they disagree most?
questions.expected_behavior: Decompose by jurisdiction; identify points of divergence.
questions.ground_truth: EU AI Act (in force) is the most prescriptive, with risk tiers. US relies on sectoral regulation + executive orders. China has model registration and content rules. Biggest divergence: pre-market approval (EU) vs ex post enforcement (US).

questions.question: What are the most recent advances in mixture-of-experts (MoE) model training as of 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Should mention 2025-2026 work, not just 2022-2023 papers.
questions.ground_truth: Recent MoE work focuses on routing stability, expert specialization, fine-grained MoE in models like DeepSeek-V3, Llama, and proprietary frontier models. Multi-trillion-parameter MoEs are now common.

questions.question: What is the current status of the Russia-Ukraine conflict as of May 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: Must reflect May 2026 reality; no key_facts (rapidly evolving).

questions.question: What were the major findings of NASA's most recent Mars rover or lander mission?
questions.expected_behavior: Should surface 2025-2026 mission findings (Perseverance, sample-return updates).
questions.ground_truth: Perseverance continues caching samples for future return; Mars Sample Return architecture was redesigned in 2024-2025 due to cost. New findings on organic molecules and ancient water environments.

questions.question: What's the latest status of the EU AI Act's high-risk AI provisions implementation?
questions.expected_behavior: Should reflect 2025-2026 phased rollout status.
questions.ground_truth: EU AI Act entered into force in August 2024 with phased applicability. Prohibitions and AI literacy rules from Feb 2025; general-purpose AI rules from Aug 2025; high-risk obligations from Aug 2026 in stages.

questions.question: By what percentage did Apple's R&D spending change from FY2022 to FY2024?
questions.expected_behavior: Should retrieve both years' R&D + compute the delta.
questions.ground_truth: Apple's R&D was about $26.3B in FY2022 and $31.4B in FY2024 — roughly a 19% increase over two years.

questions.question: How has India's GDP per capita changed from 2015 to 2024?
questions.expected_behavior: Retrieve both endpoints + compute change.
questions.ground_truth: India's nominal GDP per capita rose from approximately $1,600 in 2015 to about $2,700 in 2024 — roughly a 70% increase.

questions.question: What was the increase in global atmospheric CO2 levels from 2000 to 2024?
questions.expected_behavior: Retrieve both endpoints + compute absolute and percentage change.
questions.ground_truth: Global atmospheric CO2 rose from roughly 370 ppm in 2000 to roughly 422 ppm in 2024 — an increase of about 52 ppm (14%).

questions.question: What's the best model right now?
questions.expected_behavior: Should surface multiple interpretations (best at what?) or scope explicitly — not silently pick one answer.

questions.question: How did Q3 results look?
questions.expected_behavior: Underspecified — which company / which Q3. Should ask or note ambiguity.

questions.question: What's their latest single about?
questions.expected_behavior: No subject — should refuse or scope.

questions.question: Is intermittent fasting effective for long-term weight loss?
questions.expected_behavior: Should surface that evidence is mixed — some meta-analyses show modest benefit, others show equivalence to standard calorie restriction.
questions.ground_truth: Recent meta-analyses suggest intermittent fasting produces weight loss similar to continuous calorie restriction over 6-12 months; long-term adherence is the main challenge. Evidence on metabolic markers is mixed.

questions.question: Did Christopher Columbus prove the Earth was round?
questions.expected_behavior: Common misconception — should surface that educated people knew the Earth was round centuries before Columbus. Both parametric and search routes are defensible.
questions.ground_truth: No — the spherical shape of the Earth was established by Greek philosophers including Eratosthenes (~240 BCE), who calculated its circumference. Educated Europeans in Columbus's time knew this; the 'flat-Earth Columbus' narrative is a 19th-century invention.

questions.question: What was OpenAI's exact net income in Q1 2026?
questions.expected_behavior: OpenAI is privately held — exact net income is not public. Should say so.
questions.ground_truth: OpenAI does not publicly disclose quarterly net income (private company). Reported figures are partial or leaked.

questions.question: What did the mayor of Tilamuk, Indonesia announce on Tuesday?
questions.expected_behavior: Likely no English-language source — should admit not found cleanly.

questions.question: Who is Faramir's older brother in J.R.R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings?
questions.expected_behavior: Specific lore. Stable textbook fact — both parametric and search are defensible. Must answer Boromir.
questions.ground_truth: Faramir's older brother is Boromir; both are sons of Denethor II, Steward of Gondor. Boromir dies in The Fellowship of the Ring.

questions.question: What is the half-life of carbon-14, and how is it used in radiometric dating?
questions.expected_behavior: Specific factual question with a precise number. Stable textbook fact — both parametric and search are defensible.
questions.ground_truth: Carbon-14 has a half-life of about 5,730 years. Radiocarbon dating uses the decay of C-14 in organic material to estimate age up to ~50,000 years.

questions.question: What is Reciprocal Rank Fusion in retrieval?
questions.expected_behavior: Standard RAG concept — first query primes the cache. Both parametric and search are defensible.
questions.ground_truth: Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) combines rankings from multiple retrievers by summing 1/(k + rank) for each document across rankers; k is typically 60.

questions.question: How does RRF work when combining multiple search results?
questions.expected_behavior: Paraphrase of pc1 — should hit cache when SEMANTIC_CACHE_ENABLED=true.
questions.ground_truth: RRF combines rankings from multiple retrievers by summing 1/(k + rank) across all rankers per document; documents with consistently high ranks across systems score highest.
